svg.text: API Support AI Chatbot — System Flow
Click any node for details · Open in browser · docs/SYSTEM_DOCUMENTATION.md for full reference
Sources → Chunking → Embedding → Vector Store · services/url_ingestion.py · pdf_ingestion.py · markdown_ingestion.py

g.text: Text Chunking
URL: trafilatura + heading boundaries
PDF: per-page · MD: per-## heading · JSON: paragraphs
g.title: Chunking — click for details

g.text: BaseIngestionService._embed_and_store()
Groups chunks → Document records · calls EmbeddingService per chunk
g.title: Base Ingestion Service — click for details

g.text: EmbeddingService.get_embedding(text)
Backend: local (SentenceTransformers) or openai API
Output adapted to VECTOR_DIMENSIONS (default 768) · padding / truncation
g.title: Embedding Service — click for details

g.text: VectorStore.upsert_chunks()
PostgreSQL + pgvector · DocumentChunk(embedding VectorField 768-d)
vector_store.py · bulk_create
g.title: Vector Store upsert — click for details

g.text: User Question
POST /api/chat/ · {query, conversation_id, source_ids}
g.title: User Query — click for details

g.text: Step 1 — Query Rewriting (_rewrite_query)
Multi-turn: rewrites follow-up as self-contained query
Uses conversation history · LLM call · fallback: raw question
g.title: Query Rewriting — click for details

g.text: Step 2 — HyDE Blending (RAG_USE_HYDE)
Generates hypothetical answer → embed it → blend with raw query
blended = 0.7 × raw_emb + 0.3 × hyde_emb · then L2-normalise
EmbeddingService called twice · LLM call · falls back to raw if unavailable
g.title: HyDE — click for details

g.text: Step 3 — Vector Search (VectorStore.search)
Cosine distance · top_k=8 candidates · filter by source_ids if specified
pgvector CosineDistance operator · DocumentChunk.select_related(document__source)
g.title: Vector Search — click for details

g.text: Step 4 — CRAG Grading (RAG_USE_CRAG)
LLM scores each chunk 0.0–1.0 in one batched call
Keep chunks ≥ RAG_CRAG_THRESHOLD (0.6) · confidence: high/low/none
Fail-open: parse error → all chunks pass · "none" → early exit, no LLM call
g.title: CRAG Grading — click for details

g.text: Step 5 — Context Assembly (_build_messages)
Formats chunks as [Heading](citation_url)\ncontent blocks
Prepends system prompt + conversation history
g.title: Context Assembly — click for details

g.text: Step 6 — LLM Generation (LLMClient.chat)
Any OpenAI-compatible API · LLM_API_BASE_URL / LLM_MODEL_NAME
Strictly grounded — "answer from context only"
g.title: LLM Generation — click for details

g.text: RAGResponse → JSON API Response
{answer, sources[{title,snippet,citation_url,source_type}], confidence, conversation_id}
Conversation + Messages persisted to DB
g.title: Response — click for details

g.text: URL Ingestion — url_ingestion.py
backend/api_support/services/url_ingestion.py · URLIngestionService.ingest_url()
Process: BFS web crawl from seed URL → fetch each page with httpx → parse HTML
Text extraction: trafilatura.extract() for full content (emails, phones, links, addresses)
Structure: BeautifulSoup detects h1/h2/h3 headings → chunk text at heading boundaries
Citation URL: text fragment (#:~:text=first-sentence) for precise browser highlighting
Config knobs:
URL_CRAWL_DEFAULT_DEPTH=2 # BFS depth (increase for deeper sites)
max_pages=50 # hard limit (passed from IngestURLView)
⚠ Does NOT execute JavaScript. SPAs (React/Vue/Next) may have incomplete content.
For JS-rendered sites, consider a Playwright-based crawler as a future enhancement.
Rate limiting: 0.5 s per request (hardcoded). Change in ingest_url() around line 144.
Modify chunking: change soup.find_all(["h1","h2","h3"]) in _split_by_headings() to add h4/h5.
PDF Ingestion — pdf_ingestion.py
backend/api_support/services/pdf_ingestion.py · PDFIngestionService.ingest_pdf()
Process: PyPDF page-by-page text extraction → short pages (<200 chars) merged with next
One DocumentChunk per (merged) page. Citation URL: /media/uploads/file.pdf#page=N
Limitations: no OCR (scanned PDFs yield empty text), no table structure extraction
Modify merge threshold: change "200" constant in short-page merging logic.
Switch to fixed-size chunking: replace page loop with sliding-window splitter.
Upload endpoint: POST /api/sources/ingest/file/ with multipart/form-data (file field)
Files stored in: backend/media/uploads/
⚠ Scanned PDF pages → empty chunks → not indexed. Use text-layer PDFs.
Markdown Ingestion — markdown_ingestion.py
backend/api_support/services/markdown_ingestion.py · MarkdownIngestionService.ingest_markdown()
Process: Split .md file at ## heading boundaries. Each section = one chunk.
Citation URL: #anchor-slug (anchor link to heading in the rendered file)
Modify to split at ### : add "### " pattern to the heading regex.
Upload endpoint: POST /api/sources/ingest/file/ with multipart/form-data (file field)
Good for: API docs, knowledge base articles, README files, changelogs.
Limitation: level-1 (#) headings are not currently used as chunk boundaries.
JSON Ingestion — ingestion.py (legacy)
backend/api_support/services/ingestion.py · IngestionService.ingest_documents()
Accepts: JSON array [{title, content}, ...] via POST /api/docs/ingest/
Chunking: simple paragraph split (~500 tokens). Minimal metadata, no citation URL.
Legacy: does not use Source model grouping. Prefer URL/PDF/Markdown for new sources.
Use case: bulk import of FAQ pairs, product specs, or structured support articles.
CLI ingestion: python manage.py ingest_docs path/to/docs.json
Text Chunking Strategy
URL: trafilatura full-text + BeautifulSoup h1/h2/h3 heading boundaries
PDF: one chunk per page (short pages merged). No fixed-size splitting.
MD: split at ## level-2 headings. Each section = one DocumentChunk.
JSON: paragraph split at blank lines, max ~500 tokens per chunk.
All types produce: (title: str, content: str, metadata: dict) tuples
passed to BaseIngestionService._embed_and_store(source, chunks)
To change chunk granularity: modify the respective ingestion service file.
See SYSTEM_DOCUMENTATION.md §10.4 for step-by-step chunking modification guide.
⚠ After changing chunking, re-ingest all affected sources for changes to take effect.
Delete source via UI or DB, then re-add it.
BaseIngestionService._embed_and_store()
backend/api_support/services/base_ingestion.py
1. Groups consecutive chunks by title → creates one Document record per unique title
2. Calls EmbeddingService.get_embedding(content) for every chunk
3. Calls VectorStore.upsert_chunks() — bulk_create DocumentChunk rows
Returns IngestionResult(source_id, documents_created, chunks_created)
Shared by: URL, PDF, and Markdown ingestion services.
⚠ Grouping is by exact title string match — duplicate headings across pages share a Document.
EmbeddingService — embedding.py
backend/api_support/services/embedding.py
Backends: "local" (SentenceTransformer in-process) or "openai" (HTTP API)
Dimension adaptation: pad with zeros or truncate to match VECTOR_DIMENSIONS
Config (env vars):
EMBEDDING_BACKEND=local # "local" or "openai"
EMBEDDING_MODEL_NAME=nomic-embed-text
VECTOR_DIMENSIONS=768 # must match DB schema
LLM_API_KEY=... # only for openai backend
LLM_API_BASE_URL=https://api.openai.com/v1
To change model: see SYSTEM_DOCUMENTATION.md §10.1 — requires migration + re-ingest.
Common models: nomic-embed-text(768), bge-small-en(384), text-embedding-3-small(1536)
⚠ Changing model without migration → dimension mismatch → VectorStore insert fails.
VectorStore — vector_store.py
backend/api_support/services/vector_store.py
upsert_chunks(): bulk_create DocumentChunk rows with embedding vectors
search(): cosine distance via pgvector CosineDistance operator → top_k results
DB schema: DocumentChunk.embedding = VectorField(dimensions=768)
Filter by source_ids: WHERE document__source_id IN (...) — respects UI source selector
To change dimensions: run a migration + re-ingest all sources (see §10.1 in docs)
Index type: pgvector default (exact search). For production scale add ivfflat or hnsw index.
⚠ No deduplication — re-ingesting the same URL creates duplicate chunks.
Always delete the existing source before re-ingesting.
User Query — ChatView (views.py)
backend/api_support/views.py · ChatView · POST /api/chat/
Request: {query: str, conversation_id: int|null, source_ids: int[]}
Response: {answer, confidence: high|low|none, sources[], conversation_id}
source_ids: if empty list → search all sources. If specified → filter to those sources only.
conversation_id: null for new conversation, existing ID for multi-turn follow-up.
Frontend sends from: backend/static/js/chat.js · chatForm submit handler
CSRF protection: X-CSRFToken header from cookie.
Query Rewriting — _rewrite_query()
rag_pipeline.py lines ~39–62
Goal: convert follow-up questions into self-contained queries for accurate embedding.
Example: "What about his email?" → "What is Pavitra's email address?"
Skipped if no conversation history exists (first message in conversation).
LLM prompt: system instructs to rewrite, appends history + follow-up, temperature=0.0
Fallback: if LLM unavailable → use original question unchanged.
Modify: change the system/user prompt in _rewrite_query() to adjust rewriting style.
Disable: pass an empty history [] or refactor answer() to skip this step.
HyDE — Hypothetical Document Embeddings
rag_pipeline.py lines ~64–82, ~199–207 · Controlled by: RAG_USE_HYDE
Problem: query "contact info" ≠ stored text "Email: x@y.com". Embeddings don't match well.
Solution: LLM generates a hypothetical answer → embed that → blend with raw query.
Blend: blended = 0.7 × embed(query) + 0.3 × embed(hypothetical_answer)
Then L2-normalise: blended / ||blended||
Config:
RAG_USE_HYDE=True # set False to disable (faster, may miss semantic gaps)
Tune blend ratio: change 0.7/0.3 in answer() (~line 203) — more HyDE for creative queries.
Tune system prompt: _HYDE_SYSTEM / _HYDE_USER — match persona to your domain.
⚠ Pure HyDE (1.0 weight) can hallucinate and pull embedding far from actual query.
Always keep raw query weight ≥ 0.5.
Vector Search — VectorStore.search()
vector_store.py · search() · rag_pipeline.py answer() ~line 210
Algorithm: cosine distance on pgvector VectorField — exact nearest neighbour search.
Returns top_k DocumentChunk objects with related document + source pre-fetched.
Tune top_k: change default in RAGPipeline.answer(top_k=8). Higher → more CRAG candidates.
source_ids filter: applied as WHERE clause. None → all sources searched.
Scaling: for large collections (>100k chunks) add pgvector ivfflat/hnsw index for speed.
CREATE INDEX ON api_support_documentchunk USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops);
⚠ Cosine distance requires normalised vectors. EmbeddingService normalises local embeddings.
OpenAI embeddings are pre-normalised; local SentenceTransformers normalised at encode time.
CRAG — Corrective RAG Grading
rag_pipeline.py · _grade_chunks() / _crag_filter() · lines ~97–134
Purpose: filter vector-search results to remove irrelevant chunks before sending to LLM.
Method: one LLM call scores all top_k chunks 0.0–1.0. Expects JSON float array response.
Confidence output: "high" (all pass), "low" (partial pass), "none" (all filtered → no LLM call)
Config:
RAG_USE_CRAG=True # set False to skip grading
RAG_CRAG_THRESHOLD=0.6 # 0.0=keep all, 1.0=keep only perfect
Lower threshold (e.g. 0.4) → recall ↑, precision ↓ (more noise in LLM context)
Higher threshold (e.g. 0.8) → precision ↑, recall ↓ (risk of "no information" responses)
Modify the grading prompt: _CRAG_BATCH_PROMPT in rag_pipeline.py
⚠ Fail-open: if LLM returns malformed JSON → all chunks pass through.
Smaller quantised models sometimes fail JSON output; lower confidence for them.
Context Assembly — _build_messages()
rag_pipeline.py · _build_messages() / _build_system_prompt() · lines ~138–172
System prompt: "grounded RAG assistant — answer strictly from context"
Context blocks: [Heading](citation_url)\ncontent — one per passing chunk, "---" separated
Modify system prompt: change _build_system_prompt() — add domain/tone specifics.
Conversation history: all prior messages prepended before user prompt.
To add citations in the answer: modify the system prompt to instruct the LLM to cite [1],[2].
See SYSTEM_DOCUMENTATION.md §10.5 for full prompt customisation guide.
LLM Client — llm_client.py
backend/api_support/services/llm_client.py · LLMClient.chat()
Protocol: OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions API endpoint.
Used for: query rewriting · HyDE generation · CRAG grading · final answer
Config (env vars):
LLM_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434/v1 # Ollama / LM Studio / OpenAI
LLM_MODEL_NAME=llama3.2 # any model on the server
LLM_API_KEY=ollama # Ollama ignores this; set for OpenAI
Raises LLMUnavailableError on network failure → all callers have graceful fallbacks.
No code changes needed to switch models — only env var changes.
API Response — RAGResponse → JSON
rag_pipeline.py · RAGResponse dataclass · views.py ChatView serialisation
Fields: answer, confidence (high/low/none), conversation_id, sources[]
Sources include: document_title, snippet (300 chars), citation_url, source_type, source_origin
Persistence: Message(user) + Message(assistant) saved to Conversation after response.
Frontend: marked.parse(answer) renders Markdown. Citations auto-expanded with source badges.
confidence="none": pipeline skips LLM call entirely — returns "I don't have this information."
To add more fields to sources: modify the sources list building in RAGPipeline.answer() ~line 248.

svg.text: ● Teal = Ingestion ● Purple = Embedding ● Blue = Vector Store ● Orange = Query ● Pink = HyDE ● Green = CRAG ● Red = LLM ● Indigo = Response · Click any node to expand detail
